vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1189: Bake the Fix-All Flywheel into Panopticon — sidebar tab, configurable orchestrator, standardized status, conversation pane

plan.title: Bake the Fix-All Flywheel into Panopticon — sidebar tab, configurable orchestrator, standardized status, conversation pane
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: Tonight's 4.5h autonomous flywheel run worked, but every piece was ad-hoc — the brief lived in ~/Projects, the status report was a hand-rebuilt HTML file, run config lived only in the operator's session with no way to swap harness/model mid-run, the conversation was the raw Claude Code TUI, and the run report had to be hand-written. None of it is a first-class Panopticon surface; none of it is reproducible by a new contributor.
narratives.Proposal: Make the flywheel a Panopticon-native lifecycle role. Add a sixth `flywheel` role (singleton, Cloister-managed) alongside plan/work/review/test/ship; ship a typed FlywheelStatus contract in @panctl/contracts; expose a /flywheel page with two panes (standardized status left, live conversation right) composing PAN-1148 primitives; expose `pan flywheel start|pause|resume|status|report` plus a Settings → Roles → Flywheel row; persist only the latest snapshot + commit end-of-run artifacts (docs/FLYWHEEL-STATE.md, append docs/OPERATION-FIX-ALL.md) via `pan flywheel report`. Hard-blocks on PAN-1148 for shared primitives.

autoDecisions.summary: Block hard on PAN-1148 — consume VerbBadge/PhaseGlyph/IssueRow/MetricTile/MetricStrip rather than reinvent them.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body explicitly states this consumes PAN-1148 primitives and recommends blocking if 1148 hasn't shipped. Duplicating them would create a second card vocabulary and defeat the design-goal-one-vocabulary.

autoDecisions.summary: Flywheel is a new sixth role, singleton (not issue-scoped), running as `flywheel-orchestrator` under Cloister.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue calls for `Settings → Roles → Flywheel` as a sibling to Plan/Work/Review/Test/Ship. The role taxonomy is the existing extension point; bolting on a parallel concept would fork the mental model unnecessarily.

autoDecisions.summary: Singleton enforcement via Cloister gate (`flywheel.globally_paused` + `flywheel.active_run_id`) — refuses to spawn a second orchestrator while one is active.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue explicitly names orchestrator-spawned-by-orchestrator recursion as a risk and points at deacon.globally_paused as the precedent. Matching the existing pattern minimizes new gate machinery.

autoDecisions.summary: Status emission via POST /api/flywheel/status; broadcast via Effect RPC stream `subscribeFlywheelStatus`; persist only latest.json on disk.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue calls out 'persist only the latest snapshot + the run report; do not store every tick to SQLite'. Effect RPC is the established WS-RPC stream pattern; reusing it avoids a parallel transport.

autoDecisions.summary: Default brief committed to `docs/flywheel-brief.md`; `--brief <path>` overrides. No inline-in-config storage.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue lists two options; markdown files in the repo are diff-able and reviewable, which inline YAML strings are not. The risk noted in the issue (briefs invisible/unshareable) is exactly the reason to keep them in repo.

autoDecisions.summary: Sidebar reshuffle (Pipeline + retire Awaiting Merge) stays with PAN-1148. This issue adds ONLY the Flywheel nav entry under the existing Operations group.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue explicitly flags coordination risk and recommends 'ideally PAN-1148 ships first and this lands as a tiny n-something follow-up'. Doing the reshuffle here would create a merge collision with PAN-1148 work in flight.

autoDecisions.summary: Provide `pan flywheel emit-status --file <json>` as the orchestrator-side helper so the role prompt avoids raw HTTP and client-side schema validation lives in one place.
autoDecisions.rationale: Role prompts should not encode HTTP semantics. A typed CLI helper imports the contract and validates before POST, giving us a fast-failure point for contract drift (hazard H7).

autoDecisions.summary: Auto-mode defaults for roles.flywheel: harness=claude-code, model=opus-4.7, effort=high, maxAgents=8, scope=PAN-only.
autoDecisions.rationale: Mirrors tonight's proven run shape. Users edit via Settings → Roles → Flywheel; mid-run changes apply on the next tick.

autoDecisions.summary: Conversation pane reuses the existing Claude Code conversation surface, scoped to `flywheel-orchestrator`. No new transcript storage.
autoDecisions.rationale: Hazard H4 — parallel transcript stores drift. The existing surface already handles JSONL session files (which CLAUDE.md flags as sacred) and PTY streaming.

autoDecisions.summary: `pan flywheel report` is the single deterministic command that writes docs/FLYWHEEL-STATE.md, appends to docs/OPERATION-FIX-ALL.md, and commits `docs(flywheel): run N`. Idempotent on re-run.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue calls out hand-writing run reports as the manual step that should disappear; a single command with deterministic output replaces it.

autoDecisions.summary: Refuse to spawn flywheel-orchestrator inside a workspace devcontainer (detect via PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON or container hostname).
autoDecisions.rationale: Hazard H6 — single-deacon-invariant rule. Orchestrator needs host git/gh/tmux; spawning inside the workspace container duels the host.

narrative.Action: Define the FlywheelStatus TypeScript interface and matching JSON schema in `packages/contracts/src/`. Shape per issue body: runId, startedAt, elapsedMs, orchestrator{harness,model,effort,ctxPercent}, headline{bugsFixed,swarmItemsMerged,swarmItemsTotal,prsMerged,awaitingUat}, activePipeline[], substrateBugs[], agents[], parked[], system{}, openQuestions[], ticks, lastTickAt. Export from packages/contracts/src/index.ts. Add Vitest tests asserting parse-then-stringify roundtrip and rejection of malformed payloads. This bead is foundation — every downstream bead imports it.

subItems.title: FlywheelStatus TypeScript interface lives in packages/contracts/src/ and is exported via index.ts
JSON schema (zod or effect Schema, matching existing contracts convention) accepts the issue-body example payload and rejects payloads missing `runId`, `headline.bugsFixed`, or `system.mainHead`
Vitest tests cover happy-path roundtrip + at least three rejection cases (missing required field, wrong enum, wrong type)
`npm run build` succeeds in packages/contracts after the change and dist exports include the new type

narrative.Action: Extend the config-yaml schema in `src/lib/config-yaml.ts` with a `roles.flywheel` block containing: harness ('claude-code' | 'pi'), model (string), effort ('low'|'medium'|'high'), maxAgents (number, default 8), scope ('pan-only' | 'all-tracked-projects'). Defaults: harness=claude-code, model=opus-4.7, effort=high, maxAgents=8, scope=pan-only. Update settings-api.ts so the dashboard can read/write roles.flywheel. Add a Vitest case mirroring config-yaml-roles.test.ts shape.

subItems.title: config-yaml schema accepts a `roles.flywheel` block with all five fields and applies defaults when absent
settings-api exposes getRoleConfig('flywheel') and setRoleConfig('flywheel', …) and unit tests cover both
Tests in src/lib/__tests__/config-yaml-roles.test.ts cover the flywheel role row including default population

narrative.Action: Author `roles/flywheel.md` modeled on the existing role files (plan.md / work.md). Frontmatter mirrors plan.md (no model pin — resolved from config). Body codifies: the singleton invariant, the brief-driven entrypoint, the tick loop (inventory → diagnose → fix substrate → emit FlywheelStatus via `pan flywheel emit-status`), the host-only constraint, the maxAgents cap respect, the scope filter respect, and the end-of-run `pan flywheel report` requirement. Cleanup of the existing `~/.claude/skills/all-up/SKILL.md` content is fair game — port the usable parts into roles/flywheel.md.

subItems.title: roles/flywheel.md exists with frontmatter (name, description, permissionMode, effort, hooks block) matching the shape of existing role files
Body documents tick loop, singleton constraint, brief location, maxAgents/scope respect, and explicit instruction to call `pan flywheel emit-status` each tick and `pan flywheel report` at end of run
Role file is harness-agnostic (no Claude-Code-only assumptions); ToS gates already enforced at the harness layer apply

narrative.Action: Extend Cloister to spawn `flywheel-orchestrator` as a singleton agent (tmux session: `flywheel-orchestrator`, state: `~/.panopticon/agents/flywheel-orchestrator/state.json`). Reuse the existing role-spawn machinery (don't fork it). Add two SQLite admin gate rows: `flywheel.globally_paused` (boolean) and `flywheel.active_run_id` (string|null). Spawn refuses if `flywheel.active_run_id` is set. Spawn refuses inside a devcontainer (detect via `PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON` env or container hostname). Pause flips the gate and stops the tmux session WITHOUT clearing active_run_id. Resume clears the gate and respawns from saved state. Add unit tests asserting (a) double-spawn refused, (b) devcontainer-spawn refused, (c) pause/resume round-trip preserves active_run_id.

subItems.title: spawnFlywheel() exists in the cloister surface, writes state to ~/.panopticon/agents/flywheel-orchestrator/state.json, and creates the tmux session via the existing tmux helper
Second spawn while flywheel.active_run_id is set fails with a clear error (not a silent no-op) and a unit test covers it
Spawn inside a workspace devcontainer is refused and a unit test covers it (env-var detection)
Pause flips flywheel.globally_paused=true and stops the tmux session; resume flips it back and respawns; active_run_id is preserved across both

narrative.Action: New service `src/dashboard/server/services/flywheel-run-state.ts`. Owns: layout `~/.panopticon/flywheel/runs/<runId>/{latest.json, report.md, opened-pr.json}`, generation of runId (RUN-<n> where <n> is monotonic), atomic write of latest.json via temp+rename, read of latest snapshot, listing of all runs (id, startedAt, status). Pure fs/promises — no execSync/readFileSync (per CLAUDE.md no-blocking-calls rule). Unit tests cover write+read roundtrip and run listing.

subItems.title: Service uses fs/promises exclusively (no sync fs calls) and lint passes against the no-blocking-calls rule
writeLatest() is atomic (temp file + rename) and survives concurrent writes without producing a half-written latest.json
listRuns() returns each run with id, startedAt, status (running|complete|aborted) derived from disk state

narrative.Action: Add `src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts` with an Effect HTTP route `POST /api/flywheel/status`. Body is validated against the FlywheelStatus contract; reject with 400 and a clear error on schema failure. On accept: update in-memory state, persist to disk via flywheel-run-state.writeLatest(), and publish to the RPC pubsub topic for `subscribeFlywheelStatus`. Increments tick counter. Includes Vitest covering accept, schema-reject (3 cases), and pubsub fan-out (mocked subscriber receives the payload).

subItems.title: POST /api/flywheel/status accepts a valid FlywheelStatus and returns 200 with { ok: true, runId }
Schema-invalid payloads return 400 with a structured error body; at least three rejection cases are tested
On accept, latest.json on disk reflects the new payload AND any RPC subscriber sees the update via subscribeFlywheelStatus

narrative.Action: Extend the PanRpcGroup in `src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts` (and the matching client transport) with a `subscribeFlywheelStatus` stream method. The stream emits the current latest snapshot on subscribe, then every subsequent update from the POST route's pubsub. Schema lives in @panctl/contracts. Add a client-side hook (or store action) so React components can subscribe with one line.

subItems.title: subscribeFlywheelStatus is declared in PanRpcGroup and emits FlywheelStatus payloads
New subscribers receive the current latest snapshot immediately on connect, then live updates
Client-side subscription hook/action is exported from the frontend transport layer and is consumable from a React component in one call

narrative.Action: In `src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts` add `GET /api/flywheel/runs` (list of all runs with id, startedAt, status) and `GET /api/flywheel/runs/:id` (single-run detail including latest.json + report.md path if present). Pure read operations delegated to flywheel-run-state service. Unit tests cover both endpoints with fixture runs.

subItems.title: GET /api/flywheel/runs returns an array of run summaries sorted by startedAt desc
GET /api/flywheel/runs/:id returns 200 with latest snapshot + report path for an existing run, 404 for a missing one

narrative.Action: Add `GET /api/flywheel/brief` (returns { path, content }) and `POST /api/flywheel/brief` (writes content to the configured brief path; defaults to docs/flywheel-brief.md). Used by the Configure dialog on the /flywheel page. Both are async fs operations. Reject POST attempts that would write outside the project root.

subItems.title: GET returns { path, content } reading docs/flywheel-brief.md (or the configured override path)
POST writes the body to the brief path and returns 200; writing a path outside projectRoot returns 400

narrative.Action: Add `src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts` with a `start` subcommand. Flags: `--brief <path>` (default docs/flywheel-brief.md). Resolves brief, generates RUN-<n>, writes initial run-state, calls Cloister.spawnFlywheel({ briefPath, runId }), prints the resolved brief path and run URL. Refuses if a run is already active (Cloister returns the gate error). Add `flywheel` as a verb under the Operations bucket in the `pan` umbrella dispatch.

subItems.title: `pan flywheel start` with no args resolves docs/flywheel-brief.md, spawns flywheel-orchestrator, and prints the run URL
`pan flywheel start --brief ./some-brief.md` uses the override path; missing brief errors clearly
Starting when active_run_id is set fails with a clear message naming the active run
`pan flywheel --help` and `pan flywheel start --help` both produce sensible output; `pan` umbrella lists `flywheel` under Operations

items.title: pan flywheel pause and pan flywheel resume

narrative.Action: Add `pause` and `resume` subcommands to `pan flywheel`. Pause flips flywheel.globally_paused=true and signals the tmux session to idle at next tick. Resume clears the gate and respawns from saved state. Both print before/after gate state. No-ops are idempotent (pause when already paused succeeds silently with a notice).

subItems.title: `pan flywheel pause` sets the gate, stops the tmux session, and preserves active_run_id
`pan flywheel resume` clears the gate and respawns; gate state is correct before and after
Idempotent: pause-while-paused and resume-while-running each emit a notice and exit 0

narrative.Action: Add `status` subcommand. Reads ~/.panopticon/flywheel/runs/<active>/latest.json and renders a human-friendly summary: run ID, elapsed, headline tiles (bugs fixed, swarm items, PRs merged, awaiting UAT), active agents count, system RAM/cap, last tick timestamp. Supports `--json` to emit the raw payload. Exits 1 if no active run.

subItems.title: Human-readable output covers run ID, elapsed time, headline tiles, agent count, RAM/cap, and last tick timestamp
`--json` emits the raw FlywheelStatus JSON
With no active run, exits 1 and prints a clear 'no active flywheel run' message

narrative.Action: Add `report` subcommand. Reads the active run's latest.json, writes docs/FLYWHEEL-STATE.md (replace) using a deterministic template, appends a `## Run N — <date>` section to docs/OPERATION-FIX-ALL.md, and creates a single `docs(flywheel): run N` commit. Idempotent: running twice on the same run with no new ticks is a no-op (prints 'nothing to report'). Re-running after new ticks regenerates and amends. Use git via execAsync (no execSync).

subItems.title: Writes docs/FLYWHEEL-STATE.md whose format matches the bc3bc537b commit's structure for a sample run
Appends a `## Run N — <YYYY-MM-DD>` section to docs/OPERATION-FIX-ALL.md without disturbing earlier sections
Creates exactly one commit with subject `docs(flywheel): run N` containing both file changes
Idempotent: second invocation with no new ticks exits 0 with 'nothing to report'; uses execAsync, no execSync

narrative.Action: Add `emit-status` subcommand. Reads JSON from `--file <path>` (or stdin if `-`), validates against the FlywheelStatus contract, and POSTs to the local dashboard's /api/flywheel/status. Fails fast on schema error before any network call. This is the orchestrator's primary mechanism for publishing its tick snapshots; the role prompt directs the agent to use this command.

subItems.title: `pan flywheel emit-status --file ./status.json` validates against the contract and POSTs to /api/flywheel/status, exiting 0 on success
Schema-invalid input fails BEFORE making a network call, exits 1, and prints which field failed
`-` reads from stdin; bad JSON exits 1 with a clear parse error

narrative.Action: Add `skills/pan-flywheel/SKILL.md` following the CLI-wrapper shape (per docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md). Documents every flag and subcommand. Add `flywheel` to the umbrella `pan-help` overview. Verify `npm run lint` (which runs scripts/lint-skills.sh) passes — the linter cross-checks every flag the SKILL.md mentions against `pan flywheel --help`.

subItems.title: skills/pan-flywheel/SKILL.md exists and documents start/pause/resume/status/report/emit-status with all their flags
scripts/lint-skills.sh passes after the change — no drift between SKILL.md and actual CLI output
pan-help (umbrella skill) references the flywheel verb under Operations

items.title: Add Flywheel nav item under Operations group

narrative.Action: In `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx`, add a `flywheel` entry to the Operations group after Agents. Icon: a `Loader2` or `Repeat` lucide icon. Active-state badge reads `live` (subscribed to subscribeFlywheelStatus presence) or empty when no run is active. Routing wired to `/flywheel`. NOTE: do NOT touch the Pipeline/Awaiting-Merge reshuffle — that belongs to PAN-1148.

subItems.title: Flywheel appears as the last item in the Operations group
When a run is active, the nav item displays a `live` badge; when no run is active, no badge is shown
Clicking it navigates to /flywheel

narrative.Action: Create `src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/FlywheelPage.tsx` and register the `/flywheel` route. Two-pane CSS grid: left 1.15fr (status), right 1fr (conversation). Subscribes to subscribeFlywheelStatus at mount. Shows an empty state (`No active run — `pan flywheel start` to begin.`) when there is no run. References docs/design/mockups/flywheel-opus.html for visual structure.

subItems.title: /flywheel route renders a two-pane layout matching the mockup's column proportions
Page subscribes to subscribeFlywheelStatus on mount and shows the empty state when no run is active
Page renders without console errors when a real FlywheelStatus payload is fed through the subscription (Playwright snapshot)

items.title: Status pane: headline tiles + active pipeline

narrative.Action: Build the top of the status pane: a MetricStrip of MetricTile cards (Bugs Fixed · SWARM Items · PRs Merged · Awaiting UAT) and an Active Pipeline section rendering IssueRow with VerbBadge for each entry in headline + activePipeline. All primitives imported from PAN-1148's shared components — do NOT redefine them locally.

subItems.title: MetricStrip renders four headline tiles wired to FlywheelStatus.headline fields
Active Pipeline section renders one IssueRow per activePipeline entry, with the correct VerbBadge and progress percentage when present
Imports VerbBadge, IssueRow, MetricTile, MetricStrip from the shared module shipped by PAN-1148; no local re-implementations

items.title: Status pane: substrate bugs + agents + system + open questions

narrative.Action: Build the bottom of the status pane: (a) Substrate Bugs Filed AND Fixed — table with issueId, title, status badge, commit SHA link; (b) Currently Running Agents — delegate to AgentCard from PAN-1148; (c) System — RAM used/available, swap, agents active/cap, main HEAD; (d) Open Questions for the Next Tick — bulleted list from openQuestions[]. All fed from the live FlywheelStatus.

subItems.title: Substrate Bugs table renders one row per substrateBugs entry with a status badge (fixed/filed/workaround) and a commit-SHA link when present
Running Agents section delegates to AgentCard for each entry in FlywheelStatus.agents
System tile shows RAM used/available, swap used, agentsActive/agentsCap, and the short main HEAD SHA
Open Questions renders a bulleted list from openQuestions[]; collapses gracefully when empty

items.title: Conversation pane: transcript + input + top-bar + run config card

narrative.Action: Build the right pane. Top bar: run ID · model · effort · context %, with controls Start / Pause / New Run / Open Run Report / Configure (all wired through the new API routes). Body: reuse the existing Claude Code conversation surface (the agents page already uses it) scoped to flywheel-orchestrator. Input box accepts a message and tmux-sends it (or uses Claude Code Channels if enabled) to the orchestrator. Bottom: inline run config card showing harness/model/effort/maxAgents/scope read from roles.flywheel; clicking opens Settings → Roles → Flywheel.

subItems.title: Top bar shows run ID, model, effort, context %; Start/Pause/Configure buttons call the matching API routes and update state on success
Transcript renders the live flywheel-orchestrator conversation reusing the existing conversation surface (no parallel transcript store introduced)
Input box sends a message to the orchestrator's session (tmux paste-buffer or Channels) and appears in the transcript
Bottom inline run config card displays the current roles.flywheel values and links to Settings → Roles → Flywheel

narrative.Action: In the existing Settings → Roles surface, add a Flywheel row mirroring the layout of Plan/Work/Review/Test/Ship rows: harness picker, model picker, effort picker, plus two flywheel-specific knobs maxAgents (number input) and scope (select: pan-only | all-tracked-projects). Persists to roles.flywheel via settings-api. A note clarifies that changes apply on the next tick.

subItems.title: Flywheel row appears in Settings → Roles after Ship, with the same field shape (harness/model/effort) plus maxAgents and scope
Changes persist via settings-api and a Playwright snapshot confirms re-load reflects the saved value
A short helper text under the row reads 'Changes apply on the next tick — no restart needed.'

narrative.Action: Port `~/Projects/ALL-UP-WITH-TWIST.md` (the brief that produced tonight's proven run) into `docs/flywheel-brief.md`. Clean it for readability: remove operator-specific paths, ensure it stands on its own as instructions to the orchestrator, link to docs/FIX-ALL-PRD.md and docs/OPERATION-FIX-ALL.md.

subItems.title: docs/flywheel-brief.md exists in the repo and reads as a self-contained brief
Brief references the role taxonomy, the substrate-fix rule, and the merge-only-human-input invariant
No operator-specific absolute paths (e.g., /home/eltmon) leak into the committed brief

items.title: docs/FLYWHEEL.md — contract, lifecycle, settings, brief authoring

narrative.Action: Author `docs/FLYWHEEL.md` covering: (1) The FlywheelStatus contract and how to extend it; (2) The lifecycle (start/pause/resume/report) and Cloister singleton gate; (3) Settings → Roles → Flywheel and what each field changes; (4) How to author a brief; (5) How this generalizes the historical `all-up` skill. Cross-link to FIX-ALL-PRD.md, OPERATION-FIX-ALL.md, and ROLES.md.

subItems.title: docs/FLYWHEEL.md exists and covers the five sections above
Cross-links to FIX-ALL-PRD.md, OPERATION-FIX-ALL.md, ROLES.md, and the FlywheelStatus contract source file resolve
Surface the doc from the dashboard's Help/About panel (or wherever role docs are linked from)
